---
title: "Python Analysis Example"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  html:
    code-fold: show
    code-tools: true
jupyter: python3
---

## Introduction

This notebook demonstrates Python-based data analysis for the thesis.

## Setup

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# Set plotting style
sns.set_style("whitegrid")
plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (10, 6)
```

## Load Data

```{python}
# Example: Generate sample data
np.random.seed(42)
n_samples = 300

# Create synthetic dataset
data = pd.DataFrame({
    'feature1': np.random.randn(n_samples),
    'feature2': np.random.randn(n_samples) + 2,
    'feature3': np.random.randn(n_samples) * 2
})

print(data.head())
print(f"\nDataset shape: {data.shape}")
```

## Exploratory Data Analysis

```{python}
# Summary statistics
data.describe()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-correlation
#| fig-cap: "Correlation matrix of features"

# Correlation heatmap
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
sns.heatmap(data.corr(), annot=True, cmap='coolwarm', center=0)
plt.title('Feature Correlations')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Clustering Analysis

```{python}
# Standardize features
scaler = StandardScaler()
data_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(data)

# Perform K-means clustering
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=42)
clusters = kmeans.fit_predict(data_scaled)

# Add cluster labels to dataframe
data['cluster'] = clusters
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-clusters
#| fig-cap: "K-means clustering results"

# Visualize clusters
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
scatter = plt.scatter(data['feature1'], data['feature2'], 
                     c=data['cluster'], cmap='viridis', 
                     alpha=0.6, edgecolors='black')
plt.colorbar(scatter, label='Cluster')
plt.xlabel('Feature 1')
plt.ylabel('Feature 2')
plt.title('K-means Clustering Results')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Results

::: {.callout-note}
## Key Findings
- Identified 3 distinct clusters in the data
- Features show moderate correlation
- Cluster separation is clear in the feature space
:::

## Next Steps

- Validate clustering with additional metrics
- Test different numbers of clusters
- Incorporate additional features
